title: Composing Knowledge Attribution via Content Hash
summary: Integrate Knowledge Attribution via Content Hash with Agent Planning Derived KB by merging artifacts, validating outputs, detecting candidates, and classifying results.
standard: Ensure seamless integration of knowledge bases by aligning workflows and validating merged outputs.
procedure: Execute merge_artifacts using a content hash to combine data from both KBs following the defined protocol with boundary checks; assert exit_code < 1 and success_indicator = true; emit completion_event on success.

Run schema validation on the merged output to ensure data integrity with automated substep verification; assert substep_pass_count = substep_total and error_count < 1; persist results before advancing.

Execute a candidate detection algorithm to identify overlapping entities by executing the transformation function on each input record; assert output_schema_valid = true and error_count < 1; log transformation_duration_ms.

Invoke a machine learning classifier to categorize detected candidates by invoking the target API with the specified parameters; assert response_status < 400 and response_time_ms < 5000; log request_id and response_code.

Execute a consistency check on classified results to ensure alignment with original KBs with validated preconditions and scoped tooling; assert error_count < 1 and output_valid = true; record result_code to operation_log.
failure_modes: Data integrity issues if schema validation fails; this occurs when the precondition for the operation is not verified before execution, allowing the error to propagate.
Inaccurate candidate detection leading to misclassification.
Inconsistent results if classification algorithms are misaligned; this occurs when required context is unavailable at execution time, causing the step to proceed with incomplete information.
verification: Merged output passes schema validation checks; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
Candidate detection accuracy exceeds 90%; assert accuracy_score >= 0.95 and false_positive_rate < 0.05.
Classified results maintain consistency with original KBs; assert data_loss_count < 1 and replication_lag_ms < 500.
